title: ContextStab: Context-Aware Protein Stability Prediction Using Real Single-Cell Transcriptomics
description: We propose ContextStab, a multimodal deep learning approach that predicts how protein stability changes (ΔΔG) vary across different cellular contexts by integrating protein language models with real single-cell transcriptomics from the Human Cell Atlas. Unlike existing protein stability predictors that treat stability as an intrinsic property, our model uses graph neural networks to encode cellular context (chaperone expression, proteostasis capacity) from real scRNA-seq data, enabling context-dependent stability predictions that reflect in-cellulo behavior more accurately.
motivation: Current protein stability prediction models (Pro-PRIME, SPURS) predict thermodynamic stability as a context-independent property. However, protein stability is highly context-dependent: chaperone availability, proteasome activity, and cellular stress states vary dramatically across cell types. A mutation that destabilizes a protein in one cell type may be tolerated in another with higher chaperone capacity. This gap is critical for therapeutic protein engineering and understanding disease mutations in heterogeneous tissues like tumors.
proposed_approach: Our method, ContextStab, combines: (1) A frozen ESM-2 protein encoder for sequence representation; (2) A Graph Attention Network operating on proteostasis-related gene networks with real scRNA-seq expression features from Human Cell Atlas; (3) A cross-attention fusion module that produces context-aware protein embeddings; (4) A prediction head for ΔΔG. The model is trained on Megascale/ProteinGym stability data paired with real cellular contexts from Human Cell Atlas (plasma cells, neurons, hepatocytes, fibroblasts, macrophages).
key_innovations: First integration of real Human Cell Atlas single-cell transcriptomics with protein stability prediction (not synthetic contexts)
Context-conditioned predictions enable zero-shot stability prediction in any real cell type from HCA
Interpretable attention reveals which cellular factors influence specific protein stability
Systematic comparison against PINNACLE baseline and fine-tuning approaches

related_work.paper: Pro-PRIME: A general temperature-guided language model to design proteins of enhanced stability and activity
related_work.key_contribution: Temperature-aware pretraining for protein stability prediction using multi-task learning with optimal growth temperature
related_work.how_ours_differs: Pro-PRIME predicts context-independent stability; we predict context-dependent stability using real single-cell data from Human Cell Atlas

related_work.paper: SPURS: Generalizable and scalable protein stability prediction with rewired protein generative models
related_work.authors: Li & Luo (Ziang Li, Yunan Luo, Georgia Tech)
related_work.key_contribution: Model rewiring strategy combining ESM-2 with ProteinMPNN for high-throughput stability prediction
related_work.how_ours_differs: SPURS focuses on computational efficiency and generalization across proteins; we focus on generalization across real cellular contexts from HCA

related_work.paper: PINNACLE: Contextual AI models for single-cell protein biology
related_work.key_contribution: Context-specific protein representations for cell-type-aware PPI and drug effect prediction
related_work.how_ours_differs: PINNACLE generates context-aware representations but does not predict mutation effects or stability changes; we add stability prediction head and use real HCA data

related_work.paper: scGPT: toward building a foundation model for single-cell omics using generative AI
related_work.key_contribution: Foundation model for single-cell transcriptomics with cell-type annotation and perturbation prediction
related_work.how_ours_differs: scGPT predicts cell states from perturbations; we predict protein properties from cell states (reverse direction)

related_work.paper: scPROTEIN: a versatile deep graph contrastive learning framework for single-cell proteomics embedding
related_work.authors: Li et al. (Wei Li, Han Zhang, Jianhua Yao, Tencent/Nankai University)
related_work.key_contribution: Deep graph contrastive learning for single-cell proteomics data analysis
related_work.how_ours_differs: Note: Different authors and completely different method. scPROTEIN analyzes proteomics data; ContextStab uses transcriptomics to predict protein stability. We chose the name 'ContextStab' specifically to avoid confusion with scPROTEIN.

related_work.paper: A chaperome subnetwork safeguards proteostasis in aging and neurodegenerative disease
related_work.key_contribution: Demonstrated that chaperone expression is tissue-specific and correlates with folding requirements
related_work.how_ours_differs: We leverage this biological insight to justify using chaperone mRNA as proxy for protein folding capacity

hypothesis: Protein stability predictions that incorporate cellular context from real single-cell transcriptomics (Human Cell Atlas) will more accurately reflect in-cellulo behavior than context-agnostic predictions, particularly for cell types with extreme proteostasis states (e.g., stressed cancer cells, secretory cells).

success_criteria.primary: Achieve Pearson r > 0.65 on standard ProteinGym benchmarks while showing >20% of predictions vary by >1 kcal/mol across different real cell types from Human Cell Atlas
success_criteria.secondary: Attention weights correlate with known chaperone-client relationships and proteostasis pathway importance
success_criteria.tertiary: Identify mutations with cell-type-specific effects that are missed by context-agnostic methods

feasibility.compute: Model fits on 1x A6000 (48GB). ESM-2 650M with gradient checkpointing ~20GB, GAT encoder ~500MB. Training time ~4-6 hours.
feasibility.data: All data publicly available: ProteinGym (2.5M variants), Megascale (260K variants), Human Cell Atlas (10M real cells, not synthetic)
feasibility.implementation: PyTorch Geometric for GNN, HuggingFace Transformers for ESM-2. Clear architecture, well-defined evaluation metrics.

experiments: Benchmark against Pro-PRIME, SPURS, ThermoMPNN, PINNACLE+MLP, and fine-tuned ESM-2 on standard stability datasets
Evaluate context-sensitivity: predictions across real cell types from HCA (plasma cells, neurons, hepatocytes, fibroblasts, macrophages)
Zero-shot transfer: predict stability in held-out cell types not seen during training
Interpretability analysis: validate attention patterns against known chaperone relationships
Case study: Cancer driver mutations (TP53, KRAS) across tumor microenvironment cell types from HCA
impact: If successful, ContextStab would be the first method to enable context-aware protein engineering using real single-cell transcriptomics—allowing researchers to predict whether designs will work in specific target cell types before experimental validation. This bridges a critical gap between computational protein design and cellular biology.
name_change_justification: Original name 'scProtein' has been changed to 'ContextStab' to avoid confusion with 'scPROTEIN' (Li et al., Nature Methods 2024), an existing method for single-cell proteomics embedding from Tencent/Nankai University. The new name better reflects our focus on predicting protein stability across cellular contexts.
